blocks:
سُورة الذَّاريات

مكيّة عَدد آيها سِتّون آية بالإجماع بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١)** وَالذَّارِياتِ ذَرْواً ** (1) By the scattering winds It means the winds that scatter dust and other things.

(٢)** فَالْحامِلاتِ وِقْراً ** (2) then the carriers of burden Meaning the clouds carrying the rains.

(٣)** فَالْجارِياتِ يُسْراً ** (3) then the smoothly sailing ones Meaning the ships sailing easily in the sea.

(٤) فَالْمُقَسِّماتِ أَمْراً ** (4) then the distributors of the command The angels who distribute the affairs, such as rains, provisions (الأرزاق), and other things.

In al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) was asked about وَالذَّارِياتِ ذَرْواً (By the scattering winds). He said: "The wind." And about فَالْحامِلاتِ وِقْراً (then the carriers of burden). He said: "The clouds." And about فَالْجارِياتِ يُسْراً (then the smoothly sailing ones). He said: "They are the ships." And about فَالْمُقَسِّماتِ أَمْراً (then the distributors of the command). He said: "The angels."

And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): The like of it. And in al-Faqih, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him), regarding His saying: فَالْمُقَسِّماتِ أَمْراً ** He said: "The angels distribute the provisions (أرزاق) of the children of Adam between the rising of the dawn (طلوع الفجر) and the rising of the sun (طلوع الشمس). So whoever sleeps during that time, sleeps away from his provision (رزقه)." And al-Qummi states that this is entirely an oath (قسم).

And in al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Baqir and Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon them both), they said: "It is not permissible for anyone to swear an oath except by Allah the Almighty, but Allah, glory be to Him, swears by whatever He wills of His creation."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): That which is similar in meaning.

(٥) إِنَّما تُوعَدُونَ لَصادِقٌ. (5) What you are promised is indeed true. (٦) وَإِنَّ الدِّينَ لَواقِعٌ ** (6) And the Requital will surely take place. (This is) the answer to the oath. It was said: It is as if He argued, by His power over these wondrous things which contravene the requirement (مقتضى) of nature, for His power over the promised Resurrection (البعث), and al-Din (الدين) is the recompense (الجزاء), and al-Waqi' (الواقع) is that which occurs (الحاصل). (٧) وَالسَّماءِ ذاتِ الْحُبُكِ ** (7) By the heaven full of paths, It was said: (It means) possessing beautiful paths (الطرائق الحسنة). What is meant by it is the course of the stars or their arrangement upon the paths of adornment (التزيين). In al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): (It means) possessing beauty (الحسن) and adornment (الزينة). And al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and He said: "It is woven (محبوكة) to the earth," and He interlaced His fingers. It was said: "How can it be woven (محبوكة) to the earth when Allah says: رَفَعَ السَّماواتِ بِغَيْرِ عَمَدٍ ** (2) He raised the heavens without any pillars that you can see)?" He said: "Glory be to Allah (سبحان الله)! Does He not say: بِغَيْرِ عَمَدٍ تَرَوْنَها (without any pillars that you can see)?" It was said: "Yes." He said: "Then there are pillars (عمد), but you do not see them." It was asked: "How is that?" So He spread out His left palm, then placed His right (palm) upon it, and said: "This is the earth of the world (ارض الدنيا), and the heaven of the world (السماء الدنيا) is upon it, like a dome (قبّة) above it. And the second earth is above the heaven of the world, and the second heaven is a dome above it. And the third earth is above the second heaven, and the third heaven is a dome above it. And the fourth earth is above the third heaven, and the fourth heaven is a dome above it. And the fifth earth is above the fourth heaven, and the fifth heaven is a dome above it. And the sixth earth is above the fifth heaven, and the sixth heaven is a dome above it. And the seventh earth is above the sixth heaven, and the seventh heaven is a dome above it. And the Throne (عرش) of the All-beneficent, blessed and exalted is He, is above the seventh heaven." And this is the saying of Allah: الَّذِي خَلَقَ سَبْعَ سَماواتٍ وَمِنَ الْأَرْضِ مِثْلَهُنَّ يَتَنَزَّلُ الْأَمْرُ بَيْنَهُنَ ** (12) [It is] Allah Who created seven heavens, and of the earth their like. The command descends between them, As for the Master of the Command (صاحب الامر), He is the Messenger of Allah and the Successor (الوصيّ) after the Messenger of Allah (may the blessings of Allah be upon them both), standing upon the face of the earth. The Command (الأمر) only descends to him from above the heaven, between the heavens and the earths. It was asked: "Is there not only one earth beneath us?" He said: "There is only one earth beneath us, and indeed the six (other earths) are above us." And al-Ayyashi narrated the like of it from him (peace be upon him). I say: It is as if He made every heaven an earth in relation to what is above it, and a heaven in relation to what is beneath it. Thus, the multiplicity (التعدّد) is based on the multiplicity of its two surfaces (سطحيها).

(٨) إِنَّكُمْ لَفِي قَوْلٍ مُخْتَلِفٍ. (8) you are indeed in a conflicting opinion. (٩) يُؤْفَكُ عَنْهُ مَنْ أُفِكَ ** (9) He is turned away from it who is turned away. He is diverted from it who is diverted (يُصْرَفُ عَنْهُ مَنْ صُرِفَ). In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): لَفِي قَوْلٍ مُخْتَلِفٍ ** (indeed in a conflicting opinion) refers to the matter of al-Wilayah (الولاية). He (peace be upon him) said: مَنْ أُفِكَ ** (He who is turned away) from al-Wilayah (الولاية) is turned away (أُفِكَ) from Paradise (الجنّة). And al-Qummi narrated what is similar in meaning.

(١٠) قُتِلَ الْخَرَّاصُونَ ** (10) Perish the guessers, The liars from the people of differing speech (القول المختلف). Its origin is a supplication for killing, used in the manner of cursing (اللّعن). Al-Qummi (القمّيّ): الْخَرَّاصُونَ ** are those who guess (يخرصون) concerning the religion with their opinions (بآرائهم) without knowledge or certainty (يقين). (١١) الَّذِينَ هُمْ فِي غَمْرَةٍ ساهُونَ ** (11) those who are in a stupor, oblivious, In ignorance and misguidance that overwhelms them. ساهُونَ ** unmindful of what they were commanded with. (١٢) يَسْئَلُونَ أَيَّانَ يَوْمُ الدِّينِ ** (12) asking, 'When will be the Day of Retribution?' When will the Day of Recompense (الجزاء) be, meaning, its occurrence. (١٣) يَوْمَ هُمْ عَلَى النَّارِ يُفْتَنُونَ ** (13) The day they are tormented over the Fire: They are burned and they are tormented. (١٤) ذُوقُوا فِتْنَتَكُمْ هذَا الَّذِي كُنْتُمْ بِهِ تَسْتَعْجِلُونَ ** (14) 'Taste your torment! This is what you sought to hasten.' This saying will be said to them. (١٥) إِنَّ الْمُتَّقِينَ فِي جَنَّاتٍ وَعُيُونٍ. (15) Indeed the Godwary will be amid gardens and springs, (١٦) آخِذِينَ ما آتاهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ ** (16) receiving what their Lord gives them. Accepting what He gave them, satisfied with it. And its meaning is that everything that came to them is good, pleasing, and received with acceptance (بالقبول). إِنَّهُمْ كانُوا قَبْلَ ذلِكَ مُحْسِنِينَ ** They had perfected their deeds, and this is the reason (تعليل) for their deserving that.

(١٧) كانُوا قَلِيلاً مِنَ اللَّيْلِ ما يَهْجَعُونَ ** (17) They used to sleep little during the night. They sleep (ينامون). An explanation for their goodness (إحسان).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): They were such that the fewest nights would pass them by without them standing (in prayer) during them.

And in al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The people used to sleep, but whenever one of them turned over, he would say: الحمد لله (All praise belongs to Allah), ولا اله إلّا الله (and there is no god except Allah), and والله أكبر (and Allah is the Greatest).

(١٨) وَبِالْأَسْحارِ هُمْ يَسْتَغْفِرُونَ. (18) and at dawn they plead for forgiveness. In al-Tahdhib and al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): They used to seek Allah's forgiveness seventy times during the Witr prayer at the end of the night. (١٩) وَفِي أَمْوالِهِمْ حَقٌ ** (19) and in their wealth is a share a portion which they make incumbent upon themselves, seeking nearness to Allah and out of compassion for the people, لِلسَّائِلِ وَالْمَحْرُومِ. (for the beggar and the deprived.) In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: الْمَحْرُومِ ** (the deprived) is al-muhāraf (المحارف), who has been deprived of the fruit of his labor (كَدّ يَدِهِ) in buying and selling. Narrated from him and from his father (peace be upon them both): الْمَحْرُومِ ** (the deprived) is the man whose intellect is sound, but his provision is not expanded for him, and he is muhāraf (محارف).

(٢٠) وَفِي الْأَرْضِ آياتٌ لِلْمُوقِنِينَ ** (20) And in the earth are signs for those who have certainty,**

Signs (دلائل) that indicate the greatness of Allah, His knowledge, His power, His will, His oneness (وحدته), and the abundance of His mercy, as it was said: "And in everything there is a sign for Him, indicating that He is One."

(٢١) وَفِي أَنْفُسِكُمْ ** (21) and in your own souls.**

Meaning: And in your own souls are signs, since there is nothing in the world except that there is a counterpart (نظير) of it in man, which indicates its indication, along with what man is uniquely characterized by: beneficial forms (الهيئات النافعة), splendid appearances (المناظر البهيّة), wondrous compositions (التركيبات العجيبة), the ability to perform extraordinary actions (الافعال الغريبة), the extraction of various crafts (الصنايع المختلفة), and the collection of diverse perfections (الكمالات المتنوّعة).

In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "It means that He created you hearing, seeing; you become angry and pleased, you become hungry and satiated, and all of that is among the signs of Allah." And al-Qummi narrated the like of it.

**أَفَلا تُبْصِرُونَ ** (Do you not perceive?)

You look with the gaze of one who takes heed (يعتبر).

In al-Khisal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq, from his father (peace be upon them): A man stood up before the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and said: "O Commander of the Faithful, by what did you know your Lord?" He said: "By the annulment of resolve (فسخ العزم) and the breaking of intention (نقض الهمّ). When I intended something, He intervened between me and my intention; and when I resolved upon something, the decree (القضاء) contradicted my resolve. I knew that the Planner (المدبّر) was other than me."

And in al-Tawhid, the like of this question and answer is narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him).

(٢٢) ** وَفِي السَّماءِ رِزْقُكُمْ ** (22) And in the heaven is your provision the causes of your provision ** وَمَا تُوعَدُونَ ** and what you are promised. It was said meaning Paradise (الجنّة), for it is above the seventh heaven. And al-Qummi said: The rain descends from the heaven, and through it the sustenance (أقوات) of the world emerges from the earth. ** وَمَا تُوعَدُونَ ** [meaning] the reports (اخبار) of the Return (الرجعة) and the Resurrection (القيامة), and the reports (الاخبار) that are in the heaven.

And narrated from al-Hasan al-Mujtaba (peace be upon him): He was asked about the provisions (أرزاق) of the creatures, and He said: "They are in the fourth heaven; they descend according to a measure (بقدر) and are spread out according to a measure (بقدر)."

(٢٣) فَوَ رَبِّ السَّماءِ وَالْأَرْضِ إِنَّهُ لَحَقٌّ مِثْلَ ما أَنَّكُمْ تَنْطِقُونَ ** (23) So by the Lord of the heaven and the earth, it is surely the truth, just as you speak. Meaning, like your speaking. Just as there is no doubt for you that you speak, so too should you not doubt the realization of that (truth). And it was read (مِثْلُ) with the الرفع (nominative case). (٢٤) هَلْ أَتاكَ حَدِيثُ ضَيْفِ إِبْراهِيمَ الْمُكْرَمِينَ. (24) Has the story of Abraham’s honoured guests come to you? (٢٥) إِذْ دَخَلُوا عَلَيْهِ فَقالُوا سَلاماً قالَ سَلامٌ ** (25) When they entered into his presence and said, 'Peace!' He said, 'Peace!' He shifted it to the الرفع (nominative case) intending permanence (الثبات), so that his greeting would be greater than their greeting. And it was read (سَلْمٌ). قَوْمٌ مُنْكَرُونَ ** (You are) unfamiliar people. Meaning, you are unfamiliar (مُنْكَرُونَ). (٢٦) فَراغَ إِلى أَهْلِهِ ** (26) Then he slipped away to his family, So he went to them secretly from his guests, for it is part of the etiquette (أدب) of the host to hasten with the hospitality (القرى), fearing that the guest might restrain him or become waiting. فَجاءَ بِعِجْلٍ سَمِينٍ ** and brought a fat calf. Because cattle (البقر) constituted the majority of his wealth. (٢٧) فَقَرَّبَهُ إِلَيْهِمْ قالَ أَلا تَأْكُلُونَ ** (27) He set it near them, and said, 'Will you not eat?' Meaning, from it. (٢٨) فَأَوْجَسَ مِنْهُمْ خِيفَةً ** (28) He conceived a fear of them. So he concealed fear (خوفاً) of them when he saw their aversion to his food, due to his assumption that they had come to him for evil (شرّ). قالُوا لا تَخَفْ ** They said, 'Do not be afraid.' (Saying): We are the messengers of your Lord. وَبَشَّرُوهُ بِغُلامٍ ** And they gave him the good news of a boy, who is Isaac (اسحق). عَلِيمٍ ** who will be knowledgeable. His knowledge will be perfected when he reaches maturity.

(٢٩) فَأَقْبَلَتِ امْرَأَتُهُ ** (29) Then his wife came forward Sārah, in a cry (صَرَّة). It was said: in a shout (صيحة) of shrieking (الصرير). And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): in the assembly (جماعة) of al-Qummi, similar to it. فَصَكَّتْ وَجْهَها ** and struck her face It was said: she slapped her forehead with the tips of her fingers, the action of one who is astonished. And al-Qummi (said): meaning she covered it. وَقالَتْ عَجُوزٌ عَقِيمٌ ** and said, 'An old barren woman!' meaning: I am an old barren woman (عاقر), so how can I give birth?

(٣٠) قالُوا كَذلِكَ قالَ رَبُّكِ ** (30) They said, 'Thus says your Lord.' وانما نخبرك به عنه إِنَّهُ هُوَ الْحَكِيمُ الْعَلِيمُ ** 'Indeed He is the All-wise, the All-knowing.' فيكون قوله حقّاً وفعله محكماً. and We are only informing you of it on His behalf. Thus, His saying is truth (حقّاً) and His action is firm (محكماً). (٣١) قالَ فَما خَطْبُكُمْ أَيُّهَا الْمُرْسَلُونَ ** (31) He said, 'What is your business, O messengers?' لمّا علم انّهم ملائكة وانّهم لا ينزلون مجتمعين الّا لأمر عظيم سأل عنه. When he knew that they were angels and that they do not descend gathered together except for a great matter, he asked about it. (٣٢) قالُوا إِنَّا أُرْسِلْنا إِلى قَوْمٍ مُجْرِمِينَ ** (32) They said, 'We have been sent to a guilty people,' يعنون قوم لوط. meaning the people of Lot (قوم لوط). (٣٣) لِنُرْسِلَ عَلَيْهِمْ حِجارَةً مِنْ طِينٍ ** (33) to unleash upon them stones of clay, يريد السجيل فانّه طين متحجّر. meaning al-Sijjil (السجيل), for it is hardened clay (طين متحجّر). (٣٤) مُسَوَّمَةً عِنْدَ رَبِّكَ لِلْمُسْرِفِينَ ** (34) marked with a sign from your Lord for the extravagant.' مرسلة أو معلمة المجاوزين الحدّ في الفجور. ** مُسَوَّمَةً ** (marked with a sign) meaning sent forth (مرسلة) or marked (معلمة) عِنْدَ رَبِّكَ لِلْمُسْرِفِينَ (from your Lord for the extravagant) those who transgress the limit (المجاوزين الحدّ) in wickedness (الفجور). (٣٥) فَأَخْرَجْنا مَنْ كانَ فِيها مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** (35) So We brought out those who were in it of the faithful. في قرى قوم لوط ممّن آمن بلوط. ** فَأَخْرَجْنا مَنْ كانَ فِيها ** (So We brought out those who were in it) meaning in the villages of the people of Lot (قرى قوم لوط) مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** (of the faithful) meaning those who believed in Lot (لوط). (٣٦) فَما وَجَدْنا فِيها غَيْرَ بَيْتٍ مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ ** (36) But We did not find in it other than a single house of those who had submitted. غير أهل بيت وهي منزل لوط كما في العلل عن النبيّ صلى الله عليه وآله. other than the people of the house (أهل بيت), which is the dwelling of Lot (منزل لوط), as narrated in al-Ilal (العلل) from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. (٣٧) وَتَرَكْنا فِيها آيَةً لِلَّذِينَ يَخافُونَ الْعَذابَ الْأَلِيمَ ** (37) And We left therein a sign for those who fear the painful punishment. علامة عبرة للسيّارة فانّهم المعتبرون بها وقد مضت هذه القصّة في سورة الأعراف وهود والحجر مفصّلة. ** وَتَرَكْنا فِيها آيَةً ** (And We left therein a sign) a sign (علامة) of warning (عبرة) for travelers (للسيّارة) لِلَّذِينَ يَخافُونَ الْعَذابَ الْأَلِيمَ ** (for those who fear the painful punishment.) for they are the ones who take heed (المعتبرون) by it. This story has already passed in detail in Surat al-A'raf (الأعراف), Hud (هود), and al-Hijr (الحجر). (٣٨) وَفِي مُوسى إِذْ أَرْسَلْناهُ إِلى فِرْعَوْنَ بِسُلْطانٍ مُبِينٍ ** (38) And in Moses, when We sent him to Pharaoh with a manifest authority. هو معجزاته كاليد والعصا. which is His miracles (معجزاته), such as the Hand (اليد) and the Staff (العصا). (٣٩) فَتَوَلَّى بِرُكْنِهِ وَقالَ ساحِرٌ أَوْ مَجْنُونٌ ** (39) But he turned away with his pillar, and said, 'A sorcerer or a madman!' فأعرض عن الايمان به كقوله وَنَأى بِجانِبِهِ او فتولّى بما كان يتقوّى به من جنوده أي هو ساحر كأنّه جعل ما ظهر عليه من الخوارق منسوباً الى الجنّ وتردّد في انّه حصل ذلك باختياره وسعيه أو بغيرهما. ** فَتَوَلَّى بِرُكْنِهِ ** (But he turned away with his pillar,) meaning he turned away from belief in Him, like His saying: وَنَأى بِجانِبِهِ ** (and turned aside) or, he turned away with that by which he gained strength, namely his soldiers (جنوده). وَقالَ ساحِرٌ ** (and said, 'A sorcerer') meaning: He is a sorcerer أَوْ مَجْنُونٌ (or a madman!) It is as if he attributed the extraordinary things (الخوارق) that appeared through him to the jinn (الجنّ), and he hesitated as to whether that occurred by his own choice and effort (باختياره وسعيه) or by other means (بغيرهما).

(٤٠) فَأَخَذْناهُ وَجُنُودَهُ فَنَبَذْناهُمْ فِي الْيَمِ ** (40) So We seized him and his hosts, and cast them into the sea, So We drowned them in the sea, while he was blameworthy (مُلِيمٌ), having committed that for which he is blamed, namely disbelief (الكفر) and obstinacy (العناد). (٤١) وَفِي عادٍ إِذْ أَرْسَلْنا عَلَيْهِمُ الرِّيحَ الْعَقِيمَ ** (41) and in [the story of] 'Ad, when We unleashed upon them the barren wind. It is said He named it barren (عقيماً) because it destroyed them and cut off their root (دابرهم), or because it did not contain any benefit. In al-Faqih, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): The winds are five, among them is الرِّيحَ الْعَقِيمَ ** (the barren wind), so seek refuge with Allah from its evil. And in it (al-Faqih), and in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has hosts (جنوداً) made of wind, with which He punishes whoever disobeys Him.

(٤٢) ما تَذَرُ مِنْ شَيْءٍ أَتَتْ عَلَيْهِ ** (42) It spared nothing that it came upon passed over it إِلَّا جَعَلَتْهُ كَالرَّمِيمِ ** like ashes (كالرماد) from al-ramm (الرّم), which is decay (البلى) and disintegration (التفتت). (٤٣) وَفِي ثَمُودَ إِذْ قِيلَ لَهُمْ تَمَتَّعُوا حَتَّى حِينٍ ** (43) And in [the story of] Thamud, when they were told, 'Enjoy yourselves for a while.' Enjoy yourselves in your dwelling for three days. (٤٤) فَعَتَوْا عَنْ أَمْرِ رَبِّهِمْ ** (44) But they defied the command of their Lord, so they acted arrogantly regarding obeying it فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ الصَّاعِقَةُ ** after the three [days]. And it was read (وقرئ) As-Sa'qah (الصعقة), which is the single occurrence (المرّة) of the striking (الصعق). وَهُمْ يَنْظُرُونَ اليها (while they looked on at it), for it came to them visibly (معاينة) during the day (بالنّهار).

(٤٥)** فَمَا اسْتَطَاعُوا مِنْ قِيَامٍ وَمَا كَانُوا مُنْتَصِرِينَ ** (45) So they could not stand up, nor were they able to defend themselves. defending themselves from it, and their story has passed more than once.

(٤٦) وَقَوْمَ نُوحٍ ** (46) And the people of Noah And it was read with kasrah (بالجرّ) مِنْ قَبْلُ ** from before these (people). ** إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا قَوْماً فَاسِقِينَ ** deviating from uprightness (الاستقامة) through disbelief (الكفر) and disobedience (العصيان).

(٤٧) وَالسَّمَاءَ بَنَيْنَاهَا بِأَيْدٍ وَإِنَّا لَمُوسِعُونَ ** (47) And the heaven We built with might, and indeed We are expanders. with strength (بقوّة). ** وَإِنَّا لَمُوسِعُونَ ** It was said: meaning, We are certainly capable (لقادرون), derived from al-wusʿ (الوسع) meaning capacity (الطاقة), or, We are certainly expanding (لموسعون) the heaven.

(٤٨) وَالْأَرْضَ فَرَشْنَاهَا فَنِعْمَ الْمَاهِدُونَ ** (48) And the earth We spread out. So how excellent are the spreaders! We prepared it (مهّدناها) so that you may settle upon it. ** فَنِعْمَ الْمَاهِدُونَ ** We (are).

(٤٩) وَمِنْ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ خَلَقْنَا زَوْجَيْنِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَذَكَّرُونَ. (49) And We created pairs of everything so that you may take admonition.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him) in a sermon (خطبة): And by His opposition (بمضادّته) between things, it is known that He has no opposite (ضدّ). And by His pairing (بمقارنته) between things, it is known that He has no peer (قرين). He opposed light with darkness, dryness with wetness, roughness with softness, and coldness (الصّرد) with heat (الحرور), bringing together (مؤلّفاً) what is mutually hostile (متعادياتها), and separating (مفرّقاً) what is close (متدانياتها). Indicating, by their separation, the One Who separates them (مفرّقها), and by their combination, the One Who combines them (مؤلّفها). And that is His saying: وَمِنْ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ خَلَقْنَا زَوْجَيْنِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَذَكَّرُونَ ** So He separated between before (قبل) and after (بعد) so that it may be known that there is no before for Him, nor after. (End of the حديث).

(٥٠) فَفِرُّوا إِلَى اللهِ ** (50) Then flee toward Allah. It was said: Flee from His punishment to faith (الإيمان), monotheism (التوحيد), and adherence to obedience (الطاعة).

And in al-Kafi and al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding: فَفِرُّوا إِلَى اللهِ ** (Then flee toward Allah), He said: Perform Hajj (حجّوا) to Allah.

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): the like of it.

** إِنِّي لَكُمْ مِنْهُ نَذِيرٌ مُبِينٌ ** I am a manifest warner to you from Him. It was said, meaning: from His punishment prepared for whoever associates partners (أشرك) and disobeys (عصى).

(٥١) وَلا تَجْعَلُوا مَعَ اللهِ إِلهاً آخَرَ إِنِّي لَكُمْ مِنْهُ نَذِيرٌ مُبِينٌ ** (51) Do not set up another god besides Allah. I am indeed a manifest warner to you from Him. [This is] a repetition for emphasis, or the first [prohibition] is based upon abandoning faith (الإيمان) and obedience (الطاعة), and the second upon associating partners (الإشراك). (٥٢) كَذلِكَ ** Meaning, the matter is like that (مثل ذلك), and the reference is to their denial (تكذيبهم) of the Messenger and their naming Him a sorcerer (ساحراً) or a madman (مجنوناً). ما أَتَى الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ مِنْ رَسُولٍ إِلَّا قالُوا ساحِرٌ أَوْ مَجْنُونٌ ** (52) Thus, there did not come to those who were before them any apostle but they said, 'A sorcerer or a madman!' [This is] like an explanation (التفسير) for it.

(٥٣) أَتَواصَوْا بِهِ ** (Are they recommending it to one another?) meaning, it is as if the former and the latter among them recommended this saying to one another until they all said it together. بَلْ هُمْ قَوْمٌ طاغُونَ ** (Rather, they are a rebellious people) This is a shift (اضراب) away from the idea that mutual recommendation (التواصي) unites them—due to the distance between their eras—to the idea that what unites them upon this saying is their shared participation in the transgression (الطغيان) that induces it.

(٥٤) فَتَوَلَّ عَنْهُمْ ** (54) So turn away from them, So turn away (فَأَعْرِضْ) from arguing with them after you have repeatedly presented the call (الدعوة) to them, yet they refused except for persistence (الإصرار) and obstinacy (العناد). فَما أَنْتَ بِمَلُومٍ ** (you are not to be blamed) for turning away (الاعراض) after you have exerted your effort in conveying the message (البلاغ). (٥٥) وَذَكِّرْ فَإِنَّ الذِّكْرى تَنْفَعُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** (55) And admonish, for the admonition benefits the faithful. for they increase in insight (بصيرة).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir and al-Sadiq (peace be upon them both), they both said: When the people denied the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, Allah the Almighty intended to destroy the inhabitants of the earth, except for Ali (عليّاً) and those besides him, by His saying: فَتَوَلَّ عَنْهُمْ فَما أَنْتَ بِمَلُومٍ (So turn away from them, you are not to be blamed). Then He relented (بدا له) and had mercy on the believers, then He said to His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: وَذَكِّرْ فَإِنَّ الذِّكْرى تَنْفَعُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ (And admonish, for the admonition benefits the faithful). And al-Qummi narrated the like of it.

In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): He intended their destruction, then He relented (بدا لله) and said: وَذَكِّرْ (And admonish) [the rest of] the Ayah.

In al-Majma', narrated from Ali (peace be upon him): When فَتَوَلَّ عَنْهُمْ (So turn away from them) was revealed, there remained none among us except that he was certain of destruction (الهلكة). Then, when وَذَكِّرْ (And admonish) [the rest of] the Ayah was revealed, our souls were comforted (طابت أنفسنا).

(٥٦) وَما خَلَقْتُ الْجِنَّ وَالْإِنْسَ إِلَّا لِيَعْبُدُونِ. (56) And I did not create the jinn and the humans except that they may worship Me.

In al-Ilal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: Al-Husayn ibn Ali (peace be upon them both) came out to his companions and said: "O people, verily, Allah, majestic is His mention, did not create the servants except that they might know Him. So when they know Him, they worship Him. And when they worship Him, they become independent through His worship from the worship of anyone other than Him." A man said to him: "O son of the Messenger of Allah, may my father and mother be sacrificed for you, what is the knowledge (معرفة) of Allah?" He said: "The knowledge (معرفة) of the people of every time is their Imam whose obedience is obligatory upon them."

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and he said: "He created them so that He might command them to worship." It was said: "His Almighty saying: **وَلا يَزالُونَ مُخْتَلِفِينَ إِلَّا مَنْ رَحِمَ رَبُّكَ ** (And they will not cease to differ, except those on whom your Lord has mercy) — did He create them for that?" He said: "He created them so that they might perform that by which they earn His mercy, and then He will have mercy on them."

And al-Qummi said: "He created them for command (الأمر), prohibition (النهي), and obligation (التكليف). And it is not a creation of compulsion (جبر) that they must worship Him, but rather a creation of choice (اختيار) so that He may test them through command and prohibition, [to see] who obeys Allah and who disobeys." And in another narration, it is abrogated (منسوخة) by His saying: وَلا يَزالُونَ مُخْتَلِفِينَ. (And they will not cease to differ.)

And al-Ayyashi narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about it, and he said: "He created them for worship." It was said: "His saying: وَلا يَزالُونَ مُخْتَلِفِينَ إِلَّا مَنْ رَحِمَ رَبُّكَ (And they will not cease to differ, except those on whom your Lord has mercy)?" He replied: "This [Ayah] was revealed after that [Ayah]."

I say: Since the creation of the universe is only for the Imam, from whom the earth is never devoid, and the creation of the Imam is only for the worship that arises from knowledge (المعرفة) which inherits further knowledge (معرفة اخرى), as has been established in its proper place, it is correct to say that the creation of the jinn and humans is only for the attainment of worship. And since all are included under obligation (التكليف), and worship is sought from all by choice (اختياراً) and as a test (اختباراً), even if not all comply due to the poor choice of some of them, it is permissible to say that their creation is only for the obligation (التكليف) of it [worship]. And since they became differing and most of them rebelled against worship after all of them were commanded to perform it, it is permissible to say that this [Ayah] is abrogated (منسوخة) by that [Ayah]. Thus, all the narrations are harmonious, not contradictory, and there is no abrogation (نسخ) in reality in the sense commonly understood from it. So let this be pondered upon (فليتدبّر).

(57) ما أُرِيدُ مِنْهُمْ مِنْ رِزْقٍ وَما أُرِيدُ أَنْ يُطْعِمُونِ ** (57) I do not desire any provision from them, nor do I desire them to feed Me. just as is the custom (شأن) of masters (السّادة) with their slaves (عبيدهم), for they only own them so that they may seek help from them in acquiring their livelihoods (معايشهم). Exalted is Allah far above that (تعالى الله عن ذلك). It was said (قيل) that it is possible that it is estimated as [if it contained the word] (بقل) [Say], so it would be in the meaning of His saying: قُلْ لا أَسْئَلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ أَجْراً.* Say, 'I do not ask you any reward for it.'

(٥٨) إِنَّ اللهَ هُوَ الرَّزَّاقُ ** (58) Indeed Allah is the All-provider, الذي يرزق كل ما يفتقر الى الرزق ذُو الْقُوَّةِ الْمَتِينُ. Who provides sustenance to everything that is in need of sustenance (الرزق), (58) the Possessor of might, the Strong. (٥٩) فَإِنَّ لِلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا ** (59) Indeed there is a share for those who did wrong رسول الله صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم بالتكذيب وغصب حقوق أهل بيته القمّيّ ظلموا آل محمّد حقّهم صلوات الله عليهم ذَنُوباً ** the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم by denial and usurping the rights of his Household (أهل بيته). Al-Qummi: They wronged the Household of Muhammad (آل محمّد) their right, may the blessings of Allah be upon them. of the punishment, نصيباً من العذاب مِثْلَ ذَنُوبِ أَصْحابِهِمْ ** a portion (نصيباً) of the punishment, like the share of their predecessors, مثل نصيب نظرائهم من الأمم السالفة وهو مأخوذ من مقاسمة السقاة الماء بالدلاء فانّ الذنوب هو الدلو العظيم المملوء فَلا يَسْتَعْجِلُونِ like the portion (نصيب) of their counterparts among the past nations (الأمم السالفة). And this is taken from the division of water among the water-carriers (السقاة) using buckets (الدلاء), for the dhunūb (الذنوب) is the large, full bucket. so let them not ask Me to hasten [it]. القمّيّ العذاب. Al-Qummi: the punishment.

(٦٠) فَوَيْلٌ لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْ يَوْمِهِمُ الَّذِي يُوعَدُونَ ** (60) Woe to the faithless because of the day they are promised! from the Day of Resurrection or the Return (الرجعة).

In Thawab al-A'mal (ثواب الأعمال) and al-Majma' (المجمع), narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever recites Surat al-Dhariyat (سورة والذّاريات) during his day or his night, Allah will set right his livelihood for him, and He will grant him an ample provision, and He will illuminate his grave for him with a lamp that shines until the Day of Resurrection, if Allah wills.
